_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: To help in reducing renal colic or to allow drainage when infection is present or when a stone prevents urine flow adequately.
Stents are commonly placed after surgery for stones (ureteroscopy) to allow healing and to ensure that swelling does not block the drainage of urine after the procedure.
Urological and non-urological indications: Bilateral Calculus obstruction

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of treatment
Discharge Summary of previous of DJ Stent insertion
Pre procedure imaging showing DJ stent (X-ray/USG KUB)

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history especially previous surgery, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advise for admission, and planned line of treatment?
Discharge Summary of DJ Stenting submitted?
Did X-ray/USG KUB imaging show the presence of DJ stent?
Was the discharge summary of previous DJ Stent insertion submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes with planned line of treatment not submitted or incomplete.
Discharge Summary of previous DJ Stent insertion not submitted or incomplete.
Pre-procedure imaging (X-ray/USG KUB) not showing the presence of DJ stent, or imaging not submitted.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details not available or incomplete.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes not available or incomplete.
Discharge Summary with follow-up advice not submitted at the time of discharge.

clinical_key_pointers: Ureteral stents are placed temporarily into the ureter to drain urine from the kidney into bladder. The stent allows drainage around a stone or can speed up healing after stone surgery.
A double-J stent is a ureteral stent with curving ends that prevent the stent slipping into the bladder or the kidney. Usually after several days or few weeks these stents will be removed.
surgical_options: After the purpose of stents is over, patients are subjected to DJ stent removal.
Usually the stents are removed one after the another, there are several methods of removing single DJ stent.
